name: Home overview dashboard unification across spending, budgets, and household activity
description: Rebuild the current lightweight home/overview page into the real top-level dashboard of the application. This is the broadest and highest-impact gap-closure feature because it needs to unify information that already exists across expenses, budgets, analytics, onboarding, and household management into one coherent first screen. The target outcome is a dashboard that helps users understand current financial state, take the next obvious action quickly, and navigate household context without hunting across separate pages. This work should stay intentionally integrative: reuse existing domain surfaces where possible, but raise the quality of the app's primary entry experience to match the maturity of the underlying feature set.
evidence: Completed frontend-first `/home` dashboard unification on 2026-05-08, then hardening follow-up in same session before PR. `apps/web/src/views/app/overview-page.tsx` now routes invite CTA to the usable household flow, replaces misleading zero/loading fallbacks with skeleton and retry states across summary, budget, and household slices, localizes role labels, adds visible month context, and raises mobile tap targets to safer sizes. Focused regression coverage in `apps/web/src/views/app/overview-page.test.tsx` now locks invite routing, localized role labels, loading placeholders, and summary/household retry states; `apps/web/src/lib/i18n/locales/vi.json` adds required copy/interpolation keys. Verification evidence: `pnpm --filter web test -- --run src/views/app/overview-page.test.tsx`, `pnpm --filter web typecheck`, and `./init.sh`. Backend summary support remained unnecessary for this first pass.
